message.content:
[Hook – 0:00–0:05]  
*Text on screen:* “POV: They use your life for content but not for votes.”  
You: *staring at camera, half-laughing*  
“Y’all realize boomers will happily duet our TikToks… but freak out if we ask to, like, vote?”

[Scene 2 – 0:05–0:12]  
Quick cuts of: climate disasters, school news headlines, college tuition pages, AI replacing jobs.  
You (voiceover):  
“They’re making laws about our schools, our phones, our jobs, our bodies, our planet…  
…but we’re apparently ‘too young’ to have a say?”

[Scene 3 – 0:12–0:18]  
Cut back to you, holding a phone.  
“Tell me how I’m ‘too immature to vote’ but mature enough to:  
• take out 100k in student loans  
• work, pay taxes  
• get tried as an adult in court.”  
*On-screen text next to you:* “Adult when they want money or punishment. Child when they want silence.”

[Scene 4 – 0:18–0:24]  
You: *leans in, conspiratorial*  
“Real reason they don’t want 16–17-year-olds voting?  
We don’t watch cable news. We’re not in their Facebook bubble.  
They can’t control us with the same crusty fear ads.”

[Scene 5 – 0:24–0:30]  
Cut to quick meme edits:  
– “OK Boomer” text  
– Clip of someone slamming a door  
– “Not you again” audio or eye-roll sound.  
You (voiceover):  
“Every time they say ‘kids these days don’t care about politics’…  
that’s them low-key praying we stay out of it.”

[Scene 6 – 0:30–0:36]  
You: *shrugs*  
“Like yeah, I’m not tryna watch a 3‑hour debate.  
But I *do* care if my school is safe, if rent is insane, if I can afford college,  
and if the planet is literally on fire.”

[Scene 7 – 0:36–0:43]  
You:  
“Lowering the voting age isn’t ‘kids taking over.’  
It’s just: if we’re living the consequences *right now*,  
we get a say *right now*.”  
*On-screen text:* “No policy without the people it hits.”

[Scene 8 – 0:43–0:52]  
You: *calling out older generations*  
“They say: ‘You’ll understand when you’re older.’  
Translation: ‘Wait to speak up until we’re done messing everything up.’  
Nah. If we wait till 18, half the decisions about our future are already locked in.”

[Scene 9 – 0:52–0:58]  
You: *direct to camera*  
“If they’re scared of a couple million more young voters…  
maybe that’s exactly why we should be allowed to vote.”

[Call to Action – 0:58–1:05]  
You:  
“If you think people our age deserve a say, not just a Snapchat story about it:  
Comment ‘LET US VOTE’ so this hits more 17–18-year-olds.  
And tag a friend who says ‘idc about politics’ but rants about everything else.”

[End Screen – 1:05–1:10]  
*Text on screen:*  
“Being ignored is a strategy.  
Lower the voting age.”  
Soft background audio continues as video ends.
